title: Options banquet a fancy affair at Fancourt
description: GEORGE NEWS - YFC's Options Care Centre...
bodyText:
Gallery

GEORGE NEWS - YFC's Options Care Centre hosted their annual fundraising banquet at Fancourt on Tuesday 21 October. Nearly 220 guests enjoyed a delicious dinner in the beautifully decorated banqueting hall with the theme 'Yielding a fruitful harvest' from Psalm 107:37.


> With Chivell Viviers as master of ceremonies, the programme highlighted personal stories interspersed with viola interludes played by Ingrid Robinson.


A five-minute video created by Jason Maggott focused on what Options does.

Phil and Michelle Pennington shared the motivation and the need for guests to pledge and give financially and the programme concluded with 'All things new' sung by Fred de Meillon.

"Options would like to thank all who gave and pledged so generously and to those who invested in the event," says Laurie Edwall, the director.


> Options has been reaching out to youth and families facing a pregnancy or HIV crisis since 1997 and is located at 54 Albert Street. All services are free and confidential and 100% of the proceeds from the banquet goes to Options' running costs.


Visit here, contact Options at [email protected], or call 044 8747414 for more information.

GALLERY: Options hosts annual fund-raising banquet

Some the staff and team members who helped make the banquet happen.

Freddie Meillon sang 'All Things New'.

Phil and Michelle Pennington on the left and Dean and Laurie Edwall. Phil is the YFC George Board treasurer and Michelle is a volunteer. Dean is both the YFC George director and the director of YFC South Africa. Laurie directs Options Care Centre, a ministry of YFC George. Photos: Kathrine Heigan Photography

Chivell Viviers was the MC of the evening.

Scan the QR code to watch the video about Options.

‘We bring you the latest Garden Route, Hessequa, Karoo news’
